pages.text: Good evening, West Orange, and welcome to our con- Council of Conference agenda. Madam Clerk, would you kindly read the opening statement, please? This is to inform the general public that this meeting is being held in compliance with Section 5 of the Open Public Meetings Act, Chapter 231, Public Law 1975. A notice of this meeting was mailed to the Star-Ledger and the West Orange Chronicle on December 1st, 2014. A notice of this meeting was also posted on the bulletin board in the municipal building, West Orange, and filed in the office of the municipal clerk of the Township of West Orange on December 1st, 2014. Councilman Cirillo? Present. Councilman Cucchiara? Present. Councilwoman Spango? Present. Councilwoman McCartney? Present. Council President Guarino? Present. Mayor Parisi? Present. So, every time a member of our town employee family decides to move on, it's kind of sad for all of us. Especially in this case with Joanne, besides having the privilege of working with her as a member of the council for a long time, going back to the '90s. we grew up in the same neighborhood together. The Stacy family was a prominent family in my neighborhood growing up. Life has taken them all in different directions, but Joanne was the last tie to this community from the Stacy family. And we're we're going to miss working with her a lot. :15,520 Joanne is the youngest daughter of Joe and Catherine Stacy, who were well-known business owners of Stacy's Menswear on Main Street for over 20 years. She was born and raised in West Orange, graduating from Mountain High School in 1983. She continued her education at Montclair State College, graduating in 1988. In 1989, Joanne was hired by the Township of West Orange in temporary position as a secretary in the Municipal Clerk's office. She became a permanent employee in March of 1990. She was appointed to the position of Deputy Municipal Clerk in 1999, working under the direction of Nancy O'Hara. In 2008, she officiated the Council inauguration in the Clerk's absence. In 2009, she welcomed Karen Carnevale as Municipal Clerk and has worked hand-in-hand with her ever since. Joanne's attributes her strong attributes her strong work ethic to her parents. od evening my name ISM at the out sure of course sure than thank you does everyone have one yes thank you again my name is Tammy Williams to the members of the Town Council it is with your I'm sorry Timmy your address please 16 Hillside Avenue thank you you're welcome it is with great pleasure that I share with you a marker initiative project of Alpac Kappa Alpha sorority Incorporated for February 16 2016 in honor of Black History Month and to celebrate one of our our own Pearls of West Orange history we would like to partner with the township of West Orange to recognize the birthplace of our founder Anna Easter Brown and erect a marker in her honor we would like to erect the marker in front of the West Orange Public Library on February 5th 2016 at a 12:00 p.m unveiling ceremony Anna Easter Brown was born on April 13th 1879 on Easter in West orang Orange New Jersey Anna Brown was the daughter of Beverly and Lori Brown Anna Brown graduated from West Orange High School with honors and was well prepare

d for Howard University the top historically black college in the nation it was a time when only one-third of 1% of African-Americans and 5% of whites of eligible age attended any college Easter was part of the original nine group of 20 founders in Alpac Capa Alpha sorority it was the first sorority founded by African-American women students it has had a continuous continuing Legacy of generating social capital for over 107 years since then the sorority has flourished into a globally impactful organization of over 283,000 colleg trained members Bound by the bonds of Sisterhood and empowered by a commitment to Servant leadership that is both D domestic and international in its scope at Howard Brown worked as the chief evening librarian while she completed classes at the teachers College department on January 159 1908 Brown along with eight other women helped to found alphakappa Alpha sorority Brown served as its first treasurer of the sorority she also composed a sorority song and helped write the final draft of the sororities Consti

tution and bylaws and during the planning sessions she documented the sorority's history for the future Brown graduated in 1909 with a B A BS in education Brown also completed graduate work at Columbia University as an educator at the high school level in North Carolina for nearly 40 years she had a critical role in teaching the next Generations with her outstanding qualifications she maintained a high academic MC standard she developed exhibits to teach the community about African-American history she helped found the YWCA in Rocky Mount North Carolina and expanded Alpac Kappa Alpha by founding a local chapter these institutions created social capital and Brown died on March 5th 1957 Brown currently has two markers registered by the historical marker database one in Rocky Mount North Carolina indicating some of her work and another marker indicating the location of her grave in consultation with our T Town historian Joe Fagan who has researched extensively her birthplace and graduating class docume

ntation we identified her birth residence was located on Mount Pleasant she attended the West Orange school that was located in the vicinity of what is now the town library and she graduated West Orange High School with honors and was well prepared as quoted in several documents Mr Fagen has and will continue to be a valuable resource providing historical background data on Brown I've also met with Library director Dave QB who also agrees the library location would be ideal as he mentioned to me upon exiting this evening he's also spoken with a couple of members of his board and they are elated about this opportunity the cost of the marker will be incurred by Alpac Kappa Alpha already already Incorporated we would like anticipating your approval your input on the style and design of the marker from the attached recommendations the style of the markers is similar to other historical markers that are located in New Jersey for the marker erection we would invite the mayor council other local and 1017 00:48:15,760
